title: Unable to write pod event
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Starting pods fails with `ERRO[0000] unable to write pod event: "open : no such file or directory" `

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Not sure, for me it occurs when starting my containers

**Describe the results you received:**

Errors are thrown.

**Describe the results you expected:**

No errors are thrown.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**



```
Version:            1.8.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
What also might be worth noting is, that I always have to set `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1002` before that it tries to use the id 1000, which fails with a permission denied, but this might also be a problem with the account I'm using, not sure if this is related, just wanted to mention it :)

```volume_path = "/home/jc2/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes"
image_default_transport = "docker://"
runtime = "runc"
runtime_supports_json = ["crun", "runc"]
runtime_supports_nocgroups = ["crun"]
conmon_path = ["/usr/libexec/podman/conmon", "/usr/local/libexec/podman/conmon", "/usr/local/lib/podman/conmon", "/usr/bin/conmon", "/usr/sbin/conmon", "/usr/local/bin/conmon", "/usr/local/sbin/conmon", "/run/current-system/sw/bin/conmon"]
conmon_env_vars = ["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"]
cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
init_path = ""
static_dir = "/home/jc2/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod"
tmp_dir = "/run/user/1002/libpod/tmp"
max_log_size = -1
no_pivot_root = false
cni_config_dir = "/etc/cni/net.d/"
cni_plugin_dir = ["/usr/libexec/cni", "/usr/lib/cni", "/usr/local/lib/cni", "/opt/cni/bin"]
infra_image = "k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1"
infra_command = "/pause"
enable_port_reservation = true
label = true
network_cmd_path = ""
num_locks = 2048
lock_type = "shm"
events_logger = "journald"
events_logfile_path = ""
detach_keys = "ctrl-p,ctrl-q"
SDNotify = false

[runtimes]
  crun = ["/usr/bin/crun", "/usr/local/bin/crun"]
  runc = ["/usr/bin/runc", "/usr/sbin/runc", "/usr/local/bin/runc", "/usr/local/sbin/runc", "/sbin/runc", "/bin/runc", "/usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc", "/run/current-system/sw/bin/runc"]
```
Well this issue solved itself after recreating that user account, I'm not sure why
I would figure this was permissions problems on /run or something like that.

I am seeing exactly the same thing, but with podman 1.9.0.


```
[mike@polly ~]$ podman run jupyterlab -p 8888:8889
ERRO[0000] unable to write pod event: "open : no such file or directory" 
ERRO[0000] unable to write pod event: "open : no such file or directory" 
ERRO[0000] unable to write pod event: "open : no such file or directory" 
ERRO[0000] unable to write pod event: "open : no such file or directory" 
```
Running podman with debug, it turns out that this is the real error: 

```
DEBU[0000] Found deprecated file /home/mike/.config/containers/libpod.conf, please remove. Use /home/mike/.config/.config/containers/containers.conf to override defaults. 
DEBU[0000] Reading configuration file "/home/mike/.config/containers/libpod.conf" 

```
I deleted libpod.conf, and voila, no more pod event errors.

@tigerpaws2 - thanks for doing the debug for us all. Always nice when the first search result contains the answer.

For me this started after upgrading from F31 to F32.
I think this is a separate bug (containers.conf related). Hopefully we'll get it fixed in 1.9.2 or 1.9.3.
